title: The One With Mrs. Bing

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Don't look now. Behind you there's a guy who can break our hearts and plunge us into depression.
jokes.setup: Warning about an attractive guy behind them
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic description of heartbreak potential
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation from casual warning to dramatic consequences, fits the character dynamic well

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, don't look now, but there's a really cute guy behind us.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I said don't look!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not looking, I'm just... okay, I looked a little. Oh wow, he is cute.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, right? But don't get excited. Guys like that... they break your heart, they mess with your head, they make you question everything about yourself, and then they disappear and you never hear from them again and you're left wondering what you did wrong for the rest of your life.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so... a normal guy.

jokes.text: Come to mama.
jokes.punchline: Rachel's predatory/confident response
jokes.explanation: Brief but character-specific moment of bravado

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hi. I'm Rachel. What's your name?

jokes.text: We should do something. Whistle. / I'm not whistling.
jokes.punchline: Awkward suggestion and immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - one impulsive, one sensible

jokes.text: I can't believe you did that.
jokes.punchline: The mortified reaction without showing what happened
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom beat - the reaction tells us everything

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God!

jokes.text: Why did I whoo-hoo? Was I hoping he'd turn around and say 'I love that sound. I must have you now'?
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous romantic fantasy she imagined
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and specificity - the absurd romantic dialogue she imagined is perfectly crafted

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I can't believe I did that! I was so embarrassing!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just imagined this whole romantic scenario in my head where he would sweep me off my feet and we'd have this perfect moment...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Instead I tripped over my own feet and knocked over his drink!

jokes.text: Get up, you Girl Scout! Up, up, up!
jokes.setup: Trying to wake up coma patient
jokes.punchline: Calling unconscious man 'Girl Scout' while physically trying to wake him
jokes.explanation: Unexpected insult choice makes it funnier than generic motivation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Come on, wake up! Wake up! Come on, you Girl Scout!

jokes.text: It's so sad. It's like he's a deer, a female deer.
jokes.setup: Complaining about calling him John Doe
jokes.punchline: Sound of Music reference - doe/deer wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting John Doe to Sound of Music

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why do you keep calling him John Doe? His name isn't even John!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, a doe is a deer, so...

jokes.text: Oh, I was gonna stop.
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dry acknowledgment that Monica should stop
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective straight-person response

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, Monica, maybe you should stop.

jokes.text: Even sleeping he looks smart. Yeah, but the dents in his knuckles mean he's artistic.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous pseudo-scientific analysis of knuckle dents
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic delivered with complete conviction - 'dents in knuckles mean artistic' is wonderfully specific nonsense

jokes.text: He's a lawyer who teaches sculpting on the side.
jokes.punchline: Combining the 'smart' and 'artistic' observations into specific career
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the fantasy building

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He could be a really smart guy who's also very artistic. Like an architect!

jokes.text: And he can dance. And he's the kind of guy who, when you're talking, he's listening, not saying 'I understand' but really wondering what you look like naked.
jokes.punchline: Specific dig at typical male behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from generic qualities to very specific, relatable male behavior critique. The specificity of 'I understand' makes it feel authentic

jokes.text: You don't even know this guy. Are there no conscious men in the city for you two?
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel obsessing over coma patient
jokes.punchline: Conscious/unconscious wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - clever wordplay with character-appropriate sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's been in a coma for three weeks. Do you think he's going to wake up?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know. The doctors said he's stable, but he's still unconscious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's better than being conscious and stable. That would just be a guy lying in a bed doing nothing.

jokes.text: I can't believe you said 'whoo-hoo.' I don't even say 'whoo-hoo.'
jokes.punchline: The repetition emphasizing how out of character it was
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces character consistency

jokes.text: Put the kids to bed for this one. Don't watch this. Weekend at Bernie's is on Showtime and HBO.
jokes.punchline: Actively steering people away to Weekend at Bernie's
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing his embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My mom's in adult films. Not like, you know, adult films, but she's in movies that adults watch. She was in Weekend at Bernie's.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I've been actively steering people away from it for years.

jokes.text: Dead guy getting hit in the groin 20, 30 times.
jokes.punchline: Specific count of groin hits as selling point
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity of the count makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You should watch Weekend at Bernie's instead. It has 47 groin hits!

jokes.text: You wouldn't think so if you were 11 and your friends were passing around page 79 of Mistress Bitch.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he loves his mom's books
jokes.punchline: Specific childhood trauma with exact page reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity - age 11, exact page number, book title. Creates vivid, uncomfortable scenario that explains Chandler's relationship with his mom's career

jokes.text: I remember page 79. The thing after the opera with the girl with the trick hip?
jokes.punchline: Joey remembers specific details, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - not only did people read it, Joey remembers specific plot details

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Page 79? Oh! Oh! I remember page 79!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You do?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah! That's where... that's where your thing... and then she did that thing with her mouth!

jokes.text: When did 'Rigatoni' get back from Rome?
jokes.punchline: Calling someone 'Rigatoni' as nickname
jokes.explanation: Pasta-based nickname fits the Italian character context

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So when is Janice coming back?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why would you want her to come back?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm going to call her Rigatoni.

jokes.text: So, his plane didn't explode in a big ball of fire? Just a dream I had. But, phew!
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning hoping for plane crash
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - dark humor delivered casually with fake relief at the end

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, did Monica get back okay?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, her flight landed an hour ago.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh good. I was worried. I mean, I wasn't worried worried, but you know, I was kind of hoping for a little plane crash.

jokes.text: Your mom was arrested? I'm busy beaming with pride.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic pride in criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific sarcasm about family embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My mom was arrested.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Apparently she's a jewel thief. I'm so proud. Most guys get their criminal tendencies from their dads. I got mine from my mom.

jokes.text: Why would she say that's embarrassing? ...I just get this craving for kung pao chicken.
jokes.setup: She said something was embarrassing after intimacy
jokes.punchline: It's just food cravings, not what we expected
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up expectation for sexual embarrassment, delivers innocent food craving

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? Food cravings? That's it?

dialogue.speaker: Nora on TV
dialogue.line: Yes, I just get really hungry afterwards.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. I thought you were gonna say something else.

jokes.text: Needless to say, he got a huge tip.
jokes.setup: Story about kung pao chicken craving
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about 'tip'
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre that works on both levels

dialogue.speaker: Nora on TV
dialogue.line: I've been craving kung pao chicken all day.

dialogue.speaker: Nora on TV
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to call that place and ask them to give me a really big tip.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I love her! I think I'm having a stroke.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's physical reaction to embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between characters' reactions

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So there I was, completely naked, right? And Nora walks in...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She just stands there staring at me, and I'm like 'Hey Nora, how ya doin'?'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no, no.

jokes.text: This is the way that I find out. Most moms use the phone.
jokes.setup: Learning his mom is coming to New York from TV
jokes.punchline: Comparing to normal mother-son communication
jokes.explanation: Relatable complaint about dysfunctional family communication

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My mom's coming to New York? From TV? That's... that's not how this works. That's not how any of this works.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Usually when my mom visits, she just calls me names and makes me feel bad about my life choices. Now she's going to do it in person AND on television?

jokes.text: I bought my son his first condoms.
jokes.setup: Claiming to be a fabulous mom
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing as evidence
jokes.explanation: Typical inappropriate oversharing from this character

dialogue.speaker: Nora on TV
dialogue.line: I'm a fabulous mom. I'm always there for my kids. Just last week, I took my son to his little league game, and I wasn't even high.

jokes.text: And then he burst into flames.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan description of his mortification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - the physical metaphor for embarrassment is concise and vivid

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My mother told me about condoms. At dinner. In front of my father. While we were eating meatloaf. I have never been so mortified in my entire life. I couldn't even look at the meatloaf the same way again.

jokes.text: And teams played sports.
jokes.punchline: Hilariously generic sports summary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of generic sports reporting. The extreme economy makes it quotable

jokes.text: Please, God, don't let it be kung pao chicken.
jokes.punchline: Reference to her TV story about post-sex cravings
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows he was paying attention and is mortified

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My mother told me she had a craving for food after sex.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I said, 'Mom, that's what the Zagat guide is for!'

jokes.text: I think you need to come out of your shell.
jokes.punchline: Ironic advice to the already outrageous Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good irony - telling the sarcastic, outspoken Chandler to be more open

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why would you say that to me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because you need to hear it - you're being ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Me? Ridiculous? I'm the most normal person in this group!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, you just wore a Christmas sweater in July.

jokes.text: I heard about the divorce. Lesbian, huh?
jokes.punchline: Immediately bringing up painful personal topic
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate timing and bluntness from this character

dialogue.line: Immediately bringing up painful personal topic

jokes.text: Well, you know what they say: Be careful what you wish for.
jokes.punchline: Implying he wished his wife was a lesbian
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about marital problems delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wish she was a lesbian.

jokes.text: But a man can change.
jokes.setup: Earlier claim about not being a shot-drinking guy
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of his principles
jokes.explanation: Quick character reversal for alcohol

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not a shot-drinking guy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm more of a wine guy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's what we figured.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But I'll do a shot.

jokes.text: Anyone want me to appraise anything?
jokes.setup: Drunk Ross trying to be helpful
jokes.punchline: Offering paleontology services at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Character-specific awkwardness - applying professional skills inappropriately

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, I'm a paleontologist! I can help! I can... I can carbon date that!

jokes.text: Well, I'm gonna go to tinker-town.
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemism for bathroom
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate childish language

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I gotta go number two.

jokes.text: Start with half a dozen European cities, throw in 30 euphemisms for male genitalia, and you've got yourself a book.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she wants to be a writer
jokes.punchline: Cynical formula for romance novels
jokes.explanation: Excellent deconstruction of romance novel formula with perfect specificity - '30 euphemisms' is a brilliantly specific number

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want to be a writer.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What are you gonna write?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Romance novels.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great. So the formula is: thin, rich, beautiful woman meets rich, handsome man. They have problems that are solved by sleeping together.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not true!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Because that's every book in this apartment.

jokes.text: It's the Italian hand-licker, isn't it? No. It's the one he's licking.
jokes.punchline: Correction from jealousy of Paolo to jealousy of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character insight into Ross's real feelings

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.line: What's bothering you? Is it jealousy of Paolo?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, it's jealousy of Rachel.

jokes.text: The girl on the cover with her nipples showing?
jokes.setup: His mom asking why she sells books
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of romance novel marketing
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable honesty about his mom's book covers

dialogue.speaker: Ross's Mom
dialogue.line: Why do you sell those books?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Because they have pictures of shirtless men on the cover, and apparently that's all women want.

jokes.text: He's just a complication you eventually kill off. When?
jokes.punchline: Ross's immediate interest in the timeline
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ross's desperation shows in his immediate follow-up question

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. How long has this been going on?

jokes.text: The guy on the cover with his nipples showing?
jokes.setup: His mom saying he's the hero
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier nipple comment, now about male heroes
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that maintains the nipple theme

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, if my mom says I'm a hero, then I guess I'm a hero.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and we all know what heroes wear.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Tight shirts that show their nipples.

jokes.text: I'll just pee in the street.
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to public urination
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate lack of social awareness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more desperate?

jokes.text: Right. No big deal. In bizarro world.
jokes.setup: Monica saying the kiss was no big deal
jokes.punchline: Superman reference emphasizing how wrong she is
jokes.explanation: Good use of pop culture reference to emphasize point

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It was just a kiss, it was no big deal.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No big deal? Monica, that's like Superman saying flying is no big deal!

jokes.text: You broke the code. What code? You don't kiss your friend's mom. Sisters are okay. Maybe a hot-looking aunt. But not a mom. Never a mom.
jokes.punchline: Detailed hierarchy of acceptable family members to kiss
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific rules and hierarchy. Joey's moral code is absurdly detailed and specific

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! There's a hierarchy here, okay? You can kiss your mom, your dad, your sister, your brother... but your cousin? That's where we draw the line!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not a real rule.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's not? Well, it should be!

jokes.text: Joey and I had discussed getting in an early morning racquetball game. But apparently somebody overslept.
jokes.punchline: Blaming Joey for oversleeping (clearly a lie)
jokes.explanation: Transparent lie that sets up the cover story

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we'd be inside right now, but Joey overslept.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I did not oversleep!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You were still in bed when we left.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was meditating!

jokes.text: Well, you don't have your racquet. No. No, I don't because it's being restrung. Somebody was supposed to bring me one. Well, you didn't call and leave your grip size.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd details to maintain lie
jokes.explanation: Great improvised escalation - the specificity of 'grip size' makes their desperate lying funnier

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We were just... at the movies.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, the 7:15 showing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Of that movie with the guy in it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We bought tickets at the window, not online.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The ticket guy had a mole. On his left cheek. Or was it his neck?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He was wearing a vest. A brown vest with four buttons. One of them was missing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We sat in row G. Seats 14, 15, and 16. The couple next to us was fighting about hummus.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We got popcorn. Extra butter. Well, medium butter. Actually, no butter at all because the machine was broken.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So we got nachos instead. But the cheese was cold. Really cold. Like it had been in a freezer for... six weeks.

jokes.text: You guys are spending way too much time together.
jokes.punchline: Commenting on their suspicious synchronicity
jokes.explanation: Good observation about their over-rehearsed performance

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You two are lying and you're doing it in perfect unison. It's like a creepy synchronized swimming team of deceit.

jokes.text: I'll have you know, Gloria Tribbiani was a handsome woman in her day.
jokes.punchline: Calling his mom 'handsome' instead of beautiful
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate word choice that undermines his defense

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! My mother is not just beautiful, she's handsome!

jokes.text: You think it's easy giving birth to seven children?
jokes.punchline: Implying childbirth difficulty as attractiveness qualifier
jokes.explanation: Weird logic connecting physical hardship to attractiveness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, my mother was a beautiful woman! She had a tough time giving birth to me, but that doesn't make her less attractive.

jokes.text: He forgot to leave his grip size. He didn't get the goggles.
jokes.punchline: More specific equipment details that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of their improvised lie with new absurd details

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We need the industrial-strength lint roller. The one with the titanium handle.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And we're gonna need at least three humidifiers. For the... humidity.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Right. Plus a de-humidifier. To balance it out.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! And one of those machines that goes 'ding' when your laundry's done.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We already have those.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but we need a second one. In case the first one breaks from all the dinging.

jokes.text: What are you guys doing here? He's not even wearing a jock strap.
jokes.punchline: Specific evidence they're not really playing sports
jokes.explanation: Good detective work with uncomfortable physical detail

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, we need to talk about something.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your volleyball team. I went to your game last Tuesday and there was no game.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The court was closed. For renovations. For two weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh... that.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And yesterday when you said you had practice, I drove by the gym. You were at the mall.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine! We're not really playing volleyball!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know! Your sneakers still have the price tag on them!

jokes.text: Really? Not even to change his pajamas?
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she wasn't at hospital
jokes.punchline: Implying intimate knowledge of coma patient's clothing
jokes.explanation: Clever way to expose Rachel's lie through specific detail

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I wasn't at the hospital!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? Because I remember you being very concerned about what gown he was wearing.

jokes.text: Mrs. Mom, your Bing
jokes.setup: Ross drunk and trying to explain
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled attempt at formal address
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk speech pattern - formal intention, garbled execution

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I'm not drunk, I'm just... I'm just very tired. And I need to tell you all something very important.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We are gathered here today to discuss the... the... the parameters of our... our cohabitation situation...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Which is to say, the... the... *clears throat* ...the dimensional aspects of our residential... spatialness.

jokes.text: You know, knowledge is a tricky thing.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why he didn't tell him
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - sounds deep but means nothing, classic deflection

jokes.text: I didn't kiss her. See what happens when you break the code?
jokes.punchline: Joey taking moral high ground despite not warning Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Joey's hypocritical moral superiority

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! You can't just slam doors in people's faces!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She slammed the door on me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's not right. That's not right at all. People have feelings, you know?

jokes.text: And there's no 'J' in 'engorged.'
jokes.punchline: Spelling error in erotic word
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - the specific spelling error makes Rachel's amateur writing funnier

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you finish your romance novel?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes! But I'm worried about one thing. Is 'throbbing member' one word or two?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Two words. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I've been writing 'throbbing membr' this whole time.

jokes.text: Just try to picture her not pregnant.
jokes.setup: Showing wedding photo of his parents
jokes.punchline: Implying his mom was only attractive when not pregnant
jokes.explanation: Backhanded compliment that undermines his defense

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look at my parents' wedding photo. My mom was so hot back then.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then she got pregnant with me and, well... let's just say the glow wore off.

jokes.text: You don't have to be awake to be my man / Long as you have brain waves I'll be there to hold your hand
jokes.punchline: Romantic song about unconscious person with minimal life requirements
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd premise - romantic standards so low that basic brain activity qualifies. Perfect Phoebe character integration

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, he doesn't need a mind... He doesn't need a heart... He just needs my love... He's in a coma.

jokes.text: All right, there, mother-kisser?
jokes.punchline: Play on 'mother-f***er' with literal meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both a callback and a profanity substitute

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, how's your mother?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's fine, why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I've been thinking about becoming a father... to her son!

jokes.text: You kissed my mom.
jokes.setup: Ross's complex explanation of his feelings
jokes.punchline: Chandler reducing it to the core issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - cuts through all the rationalization

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's not that simple, Chandler. There are layers to this. I mean, we have history, and there's the whole thing with Rachel, and then there's Monica, and then—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You like her.

jokes.text: We're rehearsing a Greek play.
jokes.setup: Caught in compromising position with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous cover story for apparent intimacy
jokes.explanation: Absurd improvised excuse with just enough pseudo-intellectualism to be believable from Ross

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We were just... we were just checking each other for ticks!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're very thorough.

jokes.text: Okay, that's my finger. Okay, that's my knee. Still doing the play.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly intimate 'stage directions'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the absurd premise with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, you want stage directions? Fine. 'Chandler slowly approaches Monica, their eyes locked in an intense gaze.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: 'He gently takes her hand, his fingers intertwining with hers as soft music plays in the background.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Stop it!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: 'Now he leans in closer, his lips nearly touching hers, whispering words of passion and desire.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, that's not a Greek play, that's a Cinemax late-night special!

jokes.text: This is so typical. You know, we give and we give and we give. And we just get nothing back. And then one day, you wake up, and it's, 'See you around.'
jokes.punchline: Treating brief hospital visits like deep relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - treating their one-sided relationship with unconscious person like a real breakup

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's in a coma. I should probably visit him.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you went to see him yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but I feel like I should go again. We have such a connection.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, the guy's been in a coma for three weeks. I don't think he knows you exist.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not the point! We're bonding!

jokes.text: But I guess it was just the coma.
jokes.setup: Thought he was different from other men
jokes.punchline: Blaming his unconsciousness for their false impression
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect line - brilliant callback that recontextualizes their entire fantasy. The economy is flawless and it's endlessly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I thought he was different from other men.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: He was in a coma.

jokes.text: You kissed my best Ross.
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'best friend' and 'Ross'
jokes.explanation: Good nervous speech pattern showing Chandler's emotional state

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Mom, I need to talk to you about something. You always said Ross was my best friend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler's Mother
dialogue.line: Well, of course he is, sweetheart.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's the problem! He's not my best friend — he's just Ross!

jokes.text: The question is, when are you gonna realize I have a bomb? Are you sure she didn't say: 'When are you gonna grow up and realize I am your mom?' That makes more sense.
jokes.setup: Joey relaying what Nora said to Ross
jokes.punchline: Dramatic misinterpretation of normal mother advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent mishearing joke - transforms normal parental advice into terrorist threat. Perfect Joey character beat

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She said, 'Joey, you need to think about your future.'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, she's dying.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, she's not dying!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She said think about your future. That's what people say before they tell you they're dying.

jokes.text: And on page two, he's not reaching for her 'heaving beasts.'
jokes.punchline: Typo turning 'breasts' into 'beasts'
jokes.explanation: Great typo joke that creates unintentionally hilarious mental image

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Rachel! You wrote 'He grabbed her heaving beasts'!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? No I didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes you did. It's right here. 'Beasts.' You typed 'beasts.'

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's a typo! It's supposed to be 'breasts!'

jokes.text: What's a 'niffle'? You can usually find them on the heaving beasts.
jokes.punchline: Monica working with the 'beasts' error to explain anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that compounds the typo absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, look at this! I got another typo in my script. It says 'She beasts down and picks up the phone.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, that's 'bends,' not 'beasts.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know it's a typo!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you do have beasts. I mean, you do bend at the waist, the knees, and the elbows. You have all those joints. Those are your beasts!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, those are bones, not beasts.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, that explains a lot.

jokes.text: Did you get to the part about his 'huge, throbbing pens'?
jokes.punchline: Penis typed as 'pens'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler delivery of absurd typo that maintains the escalation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, they made a typo in the romance novel. It says 'He ran his hands over her soft pens.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not a typo, that's a bestseller waiting to happen.

jokes.text: You don't wanna be around when he starts writing with those.
jokes.punchline: Following the pen metaphor to its logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that follows the typo to its absurd logical conclusion with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more aroused by these huge, throbbing pens?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, those are just pens.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, but look at them. They're so... firm. So ready to write.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, it's a pen.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A pen that's made love to me with its ballpoint tip.

jokes.text: Wait, I just got to the part about her 'public hair.'
jokes.punchline: Pubic hair becomes 'public hair'
jokes.explanation: Good final typo in the series - creates absurd mental image of hair being publicly displayed

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Let me see that. 'Her public hair cascaded down her shoulders.' Public? PUBIC! There's a typo!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what? It's just a typo.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just a typo? Chandler, this is a romance novel. 'Public hair' completely changes the meaning!

tmdb_overview: Chandler's romance-novelist mother comes to New York to visit him, and Joey catches her and Ross kissing.
insight: Mrs. Bing's arrival triggers 69 jokes in 22 minutes—escalation-driven chaos outpaces character depth.
